Your childs doctor or an educated staff member will always be happy to answer any of your questions. Go To FIRST VISIT or APPOINTMENTS or INSURANCE

--- OFFICE TOUR ---
As we are all aware of, dental treatment in very young patients is very challenging. We provide a fun and enjoyable dental office where kids learn to keep their teeth clean and healthy for a lifetime. We are committed to offering a compassionate and friendly environment in which to care for your child’s dental needs.

Lucky Kids Dental

--- EMERGENCY ---
If you need urgent treatment after hours , you can call our emergency number at 972-758-8777. We are always here to assist when your childs dental health is at risk. Below are tips on dealing with urgent dental situations. Broken Tooth : Gently clean or rinse dirt from the area around the break. Place a cold compress on the face in the area of the broken took to minimize lip or facial swelling.

Objects Caught between the Teeth : Try to remove the object with dental floss. You may tie one or two small knots in the floss to help remove the debris. Do not use a sharp metal object. If you cannot remove it, contact the dentist. Bleeding After a Baby Tooth Falls Out : Fold a gauze pad or clean washcloth over the bleeding area. Keep it in place for 15 minutes, then repeat as necessary.

HIPPA Notice of Privacy Practices As a first time patient, we have made it easy for you by offering dentally related forms online. Please select the appropriate form(s), print them out and bring them to your appointment already completed. By offering you this option to print the forms in advance, you will be able to complete them at your own pace and in comfort of your own home. Please print the forms on white paper and black / blue pen only.

In addition to promoting a fun learning environment, we offer the most state-of-the-art-technology available. Environments for children need to be designed with careful consideration of four basic environmental needs children have: Movement The environment needs to offer children an invitation to move within safe and tolerable limits, and every child will move to a different drummer.

Take acetaminophen or ibuprofen for pain. Call the dentist. Bitten Tongue or Lip or cheek : If there is bleeding apply gentle pressure with a clean cloth or gauze. Apply an ice compress to the injured area. If bleeding does not stop, go to a hospital emergency room. Possible Broken Jaw : Do not move the jaw. Stabilize the jaw by tying a towel, necktie, etc. , over the top of the head. Apply cold compresses. Go to an oral surgeon or hospital emergency room immediately.

If it is not possible to replace the tooth, place the tooth in a cup of cold milk, or if this is not available cool water. Go to the dentist immediately. Time is important for saving the tooth, less than 30 minutes is the best. For primary (baby) teeth Teeth are not re-implanted. Toothaches : Clean the area around the tooth. Rinse the mouth with warm salt water and use dental floss to remove any trapped food between the teeth. If there is swelling, apply cold to the outside of the face.

If the fracture is more than one-half of the tooth, contact your dentist immediately. Knocked Out Tooth : For permanent teeth Find the tooth. Handle the tooth by the crown, not root. If the tooth is dirty, gently rinse it in cold water, but DO NOT scrub or handle the root unnecessarily. Try to put the tooth back into the socket. Have the child hold the tooth in place by closing on a gauze pad or washcloth.

If you prefer to complete the forms at the office , please plan to arrive 30 minutes prior to your appointment. Print All Paperwork ( From 1 to 7 ) 1. New Patient Registration 2. New Patient Dental & Medical History 3. HIPPA 4. Dental Treatment Consent 5. Cancellation Policy 6. About Dental Insurance & Financial Responsibility 7. Guardian Consent We ask all our parents to feel free to contact our office at 972- 758-8777 during normal business hours to ask any questions.

Go To OUR DOCTORS or CONTACT

--- NEW PATIENT PAPERWORK ---
Lucky Kids Dental is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of your personal health information. We comply with federal laws and meet required standards for securing that information. Please carefully review the attached HIPPA Notice of Privacy Practices and keep it on file as it provides important details about the obligations of health plans and your rights in regard to your health information.

In addition, this cancellation / no show will result in forfeiting any future Saturday appointments. Go To FIRST VISIT or INSURANCE or NEW PATIENT PAPERWORK

--- OUR STAFF ---
Our experienced staff is especially interested in making all our patients feel right at home. We put your child’s needs first to achieve efficient and comprehensive treatment. Our staff is trained and skilled, and we strive to provide energetic and fun-loving service to each patient.

Because insurance policies vary, we can only estimate your coverage in good faith but cannot guarantee coverage due to the complexities of insurance contracts. Your estimated patient portion must be paid at the time of service. As a service to our patients, we will bill insurance companies for services and allow them 45 days to render payment. After 60 days, you are responsible for the entire balance, paid-in-full. If you have any questions our courteous staff is always available to answer them.

Each member of our treatment team is a registered assistant , and our office staff has years of experience in treating patients with the highest level of care. We strive to stay abreast of the latest on technological advances by regularly attending continuing education courses and seminars. Each of our staff members is also trained in adult, child and infant CPR & First Aid.

1st Cancellation : Someone from our office will notify you of your missed appointment to determine if there were any extenuation circumstances. 2nd Cancellation : A fee of $25 will be charged to you. It will not be billed to your insurance. 3rd Cancellation : At this time, the patient will be dismissed from the practice. Saturday Cancellation Policy : A fee of $50 will be charged to you for not giving a 48hour notice or no show.

We will always be happy to answer any of your questions. Go To FIRST VISIT or APPOINTMENTS or NEW PATIENT PAPERWORK

--- APPOINTMENTS ---
Your scheduled appointment time has been reserved specifically for you. Appointment Policy As a courtesy to other scheduled patients, if you are late 10 minutes or more than your scheduled appointment, your appointment may need to be rescheduled. Cancellation Policy We request 48-hours notice if you need to cancel your appointment.

We ask all our parents to feel free to contact our office at 972- 758-8777 during normal business hours to ask any questions. Your childs doctor or an educated staff member will always be happy to answer any of your questions. Go To OUR DOCTORS or OFFICE TOUR or CONTACT

--- CONTACT ---
We are always available to talk with you and address your concerns. Please feel free to contact us during work hours through our office phone number or e-mail address.

If too restricted, children become frustrated and fidgety, or they try to gain access to prohibited components of the environment. Comfort A feeling of comfort is important to childrens use an exploration of the environment. There need to be moderate and varied levels of stimulation for all the senses. Behavior is optimized at a comfort zone of stimulation, neither too little or too much.

The doctors at Lucky Kids Dental are contracted as a preferred provider with PPO dental insurance plans listed below. We are not contracted with any HMO plans. Aetna / Assurant / Ameritas / Blue Cross Blue Shield Cigna / Careington / Delta Dental / Denta Max Guardian / Humana / Metlife / Principal / United Health Care Lincoln / Medicaid & CHIP We ask all our parents to feel free to contact our office at 972- 758-8777 during normal business hours to ask any questions.

An overload of sensory stimulation and noise will exacerbate childrens feelings of discomfort and result in undesired behaviors. Competence Children need to feel successful in negotiating the environment. Yet the world at large forces them to constantly confront intimidating and frustrating experiences. Successful childrens environments are designed to make children competent inhabitants and users.

Control Children need the ability to exercise control over the environment and acquire increased levels of autonomy. Children must have experiences that allow them to experiment and make decisions. Go To OUR DOCTORS or FIRST VISIT or APPOINTMENTS or INSURANCE or NEW PATIENT PAPERWORK

--- INSURANCE ---
Our office is committed to helping you maximize your insurance benefits.

Here are some tips to help prepare your child for their first visit: Expect your child to have a pleasant experience. It is best if you can refrain from using words around your child that might cause unnecessary fear and anxiety, such as “needle, pull, drill or hurt. ” Our office makes a practice of using words that convey the same message but are pleasant and not frightening to the child. If your child is suffering from dental pain (a toothache, etc. ) emergency treatment will be given.

Good dental care is an important part of your child’s overall health. We strive to provide a positive dental experience for you and your child. You can make the first visit to the dentist enjoyable and positive. The mission of LKD is to provide the highest quality and compassionate dental care to our patients. We will achieve this mission through continuing education and committing ourselves to provide dental care in a gentle and caring manner.

Once the baby tooth is out, the tongue usually moves the permanent tooth forward into its proper position, if space permits. If this crowded condition should occur in the upper jaw, the baby teeth should be removed immediately. My two-year-old child has a cavity in a tooth. Should the tooth be filled. Yes, it should be treated as soon as possible to avoid pain and infection.

We ask all our parents to feel free to contact our office at 972- 758-8777 during normal business hours to ask any questions. Your childs doctor or an educated staff member will always be happy to answer any of your questions. Go To OUR DOCTORS or FIRST VISIT or APPOINTMENTS or INSURANCE or NEW PATIENT PAPERWORK

--- OUR OFFICE ---
Welcome to Lucky Kids Dental. Our professional and friendly staff is here to ensure that your child’s visit is a fun and positive experience.

As the tooth loosens, it is natural for a child to assist the process, which may take several weeks. My child has a double row of front teeth. Why is this and what should be done about it. This is common. Sometimes the permanent teeth begin to come in before the baby teeth are lost. When this happens, unless the child can work the baby teeth out by himself within a few weeks, the pediatric dentist may have to remove them.

At LKD, we are committed to the specialty of childrens dentistry. Our patients safety and comfort are our top priorities. Our entire staff is specifically trained in the treatment of infants, children, adolescents and patients with special health care needs. We are committed to making your childs first and subsequent visits as pleasant and comfortable as possible.

At LKD, we offer comprehensive dental treatment options for your childs dental needs ranging from traditional behavior guidance to general anesthesia. We offer the most advanced pediatric procedures and equipment available to ensure that every child gets the highest standard of dental care. Upon meeting and assessing your childs dental needs and his/her behavior, LKD will present treatment options best suited for your child and answer any questions you may have as a parent.

The following are some of the dental services that are offered at LKD. Preventive Dentistry Traditional Dental Treatment without Sedation Nitrous Oxide (Laughing gas) Oral Conscious Sedation/IV sedation General Anesthesia Dental Emergency Triage Laser Dentistry Go To OUR STAFF or OFFICE TOUR or EMERGENCY

--- OUR DOCTORS ---
PEDIATRIC DENTISTRY Dr. Sara Van Arsdall Dr. Sara is a Board Certified Specialist in Pediatric Dentistry. A passion for children leaves her blessed to love her job.
Kim is one of the few dentists who are able to perform all levels of anesthesia. For more information about Dr. Kim, please visit anesthesiafordds. com or caredentistrytx.

Professional Societies : American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry / American Dental Association / Texas Dental Association Education : Residency at NYU Lutheran Medical Center in Hawaii in Oahu, HI, Certificate of Pediatric dentistry 2015 / Harvard School of Dental Medicine in Boston, MA, Doctor of Dental Medicine 2013 Board Certifications : A Diplomate of American Board of Pediatric Dentistry 2016 Languages Spoken : English, Some Spanish, Some Korean Dr. Kee-won Kwon Dr.

Comforting kids and motivating them through treatment and to improve their dental habits is her favorite part of the job. As a mom, treating each child as if it were her own drives each treatment decision. She is dedicated to offering the highest level of treatment, and loves being a part of a premier private pediatric practice in a world where healthcare is becoming increasingly corporate and detached.
Here at Lucky Kids Dental the only ones making decisions about your child’s care are you and the doctors you see.

Kwon is a Board Certified Specialist in Pediatric Dentistry. As a pediatric dental specialist, her main goal is that each and every child seen at Lucky Kids Dental to have a positive dental experience and be excited to come back for their subsequent dental visits. Dr. Kwon is offering pediatric dental care that is thoughtful, careful, ethical and based on the most recent scientific evidence.

Patenaude is Dedicated to Excellence in Orthodontics During his time at Harvard, he served as a national leader of the American Student Dental Association advocating for policies to maintain a high-quality standard of care. Dr. Patenaude is an instructor for the Charles H Tweed International Foundation. This is a group dedicated to the pursuit of excellence in orthodontic diagnosis and treatment. ANESTHESIOLOGIST Dr. Jinsoo Kim Dr. Kim is a Board Certified Dentist Anesthesiologist. Dr.

Professional Societies : American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry / American Dental Association / Texas Dental Association Education : Residency at Childrens Hospital of Wisconsin in Milwaukee, WI, Certificate of Pediatric dentistry 2010 / Marquette University School of Dentistry in Milwaukee, WI, Doctor of Dental Surgery 2001 Board Certifications : A Diplomate of American Board of Pediatric Dentistry 2011 Languages Spoken : English, Spanish, Korean ORTHODONTIC DENTISTRY Dr. Adam Patenaude Dr.

com Professional Societies : American Society of Dentist Anesthesiologist / American Dental Board of Anesthesiology / International Anesthesia Research Society / American Society of Anesthesiology / Society of Pediatric Anesthesia / Society for Ambulatory Anesthesia / American Dental Association / Texas Dental Association Education : Loma Linda University School of Dentistry, Dental Anesthesiology 2011 / University of Texas Health and Science Center in San Antonio, General Practice Residency 2006 / University of Washington School of Dentistry, Doctor of Dental Surgery 2005 Certifications : Board Certified Dentist Anesthesiology by American Dental Board of Anesthesiology / Certification in BLS, ACLS, PALS / Instructor for BLS and ACLS State Licenses : Texas, Washington Languages Spoken : English, Korean

--- Hello world.

Adam Patenaude is trained as both a chemical engineer and an orthodontist. He chose orthodontics because it perfectly combines his love of physics & esthetics with the greatest reward of transforming smiles and lives. He completed his dental training at Harvard University and received his Orthodontic Specialty Certificate & Masters of Science in Oral Biology at Texas A&M/Baylor College of Dentistry, one of the leading orthodontic programs in the country. Prior to dental school, Dr.

Patenaude graduated as valedictorian at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute where he studied chemical engineering. His background uniquely qualifies him to treat the most complex orthodontic cases. Outside of orthodontics Dr. Patenaude enjoys spending time with his wife & daughter, playing golf, pickleball, board games, and being involved in his church. Dr.

A : Pediatric dentists are the pediatricians of dentistry.

A pediatric dentist has two to three years of specialty training following dental school and limits his/her practice to treating children only. Pediatric dentists are primary and specialty oral care providers for infants and children through adolescence, including those with special health needs. Q : Is my child too ill for their dental appointment.

Lead aprons and high-speed film are used to ensure safety and minimize the amount of radiation. Will X-rays be taken at every appointment. No. Pediatric dentists, acting in accordance with the guidelines from the American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry, recommend X-rays only when necessary to protect your childs dental health. For example, X-rays may be needed to diagnose tooth decay or abnormalities. Or, they may be required for orthodontic treatment.

Parents should take their children to the dentist regularly, beginning with the eruption of the first tooth. Then, the dentist can recommend a specific program of brushing, flossing, and other treatments for parents to supervise and teach to their children. These home treatments, when added to regular dental visits and a balanced diet, will help give your child a lifetime of healthy habits. How do dental sealants work. Sealants work by filling in the crevasses on the chewing surfaces of the teeth.

Encourage your child to drink from a cup as they approach their first birthday. Children should not fall asleep with a bottle or cup. At-will nighttime breast-feeding should be avoided after the first primary (baby) teeth begin to erupt. Drinking juice from a bottle should be avoided. When the juice is offered, it should be in a cup. Juice and milk should never be given to children when they are going to bed. How can parents help prevent tooth decay.

This prevents food particles to get caught in the teeth, causing cavities. How often does my child need to see the pediatric dentist. A check-up every six months is recommended in order to prevent cavities and other dental problems. However, your pediatric dentist can tell you when and how often your child should visit based on their personal oral condition. Is it all right for my child to remove her own baby teeth when they become loose. This is perfectly all right.

If we find cavities or other problems, these things can be taken care of early before they become a bigger problem. How can I prevent decay caused by nursing. Avoid nursing children to sleep or putting anything other than water in their bedtime bottle. Also, learn the proper way to brush and floss your childs teeth. Take your child to a pediatric dentist regularly to have his/her teeth and gums checked. The first dental visit should be scheduled for your childs first birthday.

Your pediatric dentist will discuss the need for X-rays with you before any are taken. Are thumb sucking and pacifier habits harmful to a childs teeth. Thumb and pacifier sucking habits will generally only become a problem if they go on for a very long period of time. Most children stop these habits on their own, but if they are still sucking their thumbs or fingers when the permanent teeth arrive, your pediatric dentist may recommend a mouth appliance.

Are baby teeth really that important to my child. Baby teeth are important for many reasons. Not only do they help children speak clearly and chew naturally, but they also aid in forming a path those permanent teeth can follow when they are ready to erupt. How safe are dental X-rays. There is very little risk in dental X-rays. Pediatric dentists are especially careful to limit the amount of radiation to which children are exposed.

At what age should I bring my child to the pediatric dentist. Children should have the first dental visit within six months of the eruption of the first baby tooth and no later than their first birthday. This is so that an assessment and record can be made of your childs dental development and risk of getting cavities. This also gives us the opportunity to discuss good oral hygiene practices at home, diet, injury prevention and the possible need for fluoride supplements.

Once the baby tooth is out, the tongue usually moves the permanent tooth forward into its proper position, if space permits. If this crowded condition should occur in the upper jaw, the baby teeth should be removed immediately. My two-year-old child has a cavity in a tooth. Should the tooth be filled. Yes, it should be treated as soon as possible to avoid pain and infection.

As the tooth loosens, it is natural for a child to assist the process, which may take several weeks. My child has a double row of front teeth. Why is this and what should be done about it. This is common. Sometimes the permanent teeth begin to come in before the baby teeth are lost. When this happens, unless the child can work the baby teeth out by himself within a few weeks, the pediatric dentist may have to remove them.

A pediatric dentist has two to three years of specialty training following dental school and limits his/her practice to treating children only. Pediatric dentists are primary and specialty oral care providers for infants and children through adolescence, including those with special health needs. A : If your child has a fever, vomiting, diarrhea, excessive coughing, or they were unable to attend school or daycare due to illness on the day of their appointment, we request that you please call us at 972-758-8777 to reschedule.
